message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Israel led a rapid vaccine rollout against COVID-19, leading to a local remission of the epidemic and rolling back of most public health measures. Further vaccination of 12-15-year-olds may be hindered by public perceptions of the necessity and safety of vaccination.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>we examined the considerations of vaccine hesitant parents (VHPs) regarding vaccination of children against COVID-19. The responses of 456 parents were surveyed and analyzed before FDA authorization of vaccination of children.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>parents who were vaccinated against COVID-19 were more likely to intend to vaccinate their children (r=-0.466, p<0.01). Low accessibility of vaccination may be a dissuading factor for VHPs more inclined to vaccinate. Vaccine efficacy and gaining a “Green Pass” were positively associated with an intention to vaccinate and statistically significant. VHPs inclined not to vaccinate indicated short development time and possible long term effects as dissuading factors.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>vaccine promotion should be tailored for VHP’s positive and negative considerations for higher uptake.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Vaccine-Hesitant Parents’ Considerations Regarding Covid-19 Vaccination of Adolescents

reference.unstructured: Israel Ministry of Health Covid-19 Dashboard. https://datadashboard.health.gov.il/COVID-19/general. Accessed May 14, 2021.
Israeli Central Bureau of Statistics census data. https://www.cbs.gov.il/he/publications/doclib/2019/2.shnatonpopulation/st02_03.pdf. Accessed May 14, 2021.
What is a Green Pass. https://corona.health.gov.il/en/directives/green-pass-info/. Accessed May 19, 2021.
